(Prospective) Tenant Life Support!
ran into friend mine recently who helps manage very large portfolio rental properties. She always has the funniest stories tell, and just had pass this one on.
They had running for rental property nice area town. was typical bedroom bread and butter house and there had been lot interest this particular property. She said that until just before closing, she had the usual run the mill tenant applications.
Then the very last minute before closing, lady ran into the office waving cash and said she had put her application today. She told the property manager that she was going have move quickly she currently lived two story house with the bedrooms the second floor. The applicant said this house was the right location, and she reiterated that needed least one first floor bedroom. She said this house would perfect!
The prospective tenant began filling out the application and when was complete my, friend looked over everything noting that the income given was for her and her husband. Wanting get much information possible, she asked her why she needed move quickly. This nice lady said that, "Her husband would off life support any day now, and when came home from the hospital was going need first floor bedroom that would accommodate his hospital bed would need for his long recovery".
After friend recovered from the shock hearing was currently ICU life support, she gently asked the applicant what she would some chance didn't recover. The tenant said she was sure was going recover but might take some time. She just needed house with first floor bedroom. friend pointed out that she didn't have sufficient income herself rent the property.
how did they leave it?
friend the property manager told her see she could find co-signer and get back with her. She never heard back from the lady really don't know what happened.
Wow, that's sad situation. But makes sense that you need rent someone who can pay. suspect she probably found another home rent hopefully works out for her and her husband (and the landlord).
hope too Mike. the end the day, you have have tenant that can pay the rent that you can pay for the house. would much worse let them move (knowing that they couldn't pay) and then have evict them. one wants that.

Redding Music Producer and Artist LAEL, Jeff Schneeweis, and his wife Hayes, along with their three girls, and pets, have lost their entire home the Carr fire.
The news was confirmed the early morning Saturday, July 28, 2018. They have lost their entire home and all their belongings, including most Jeff's home studio equipment and favorite instruments. Thankful for their lives and their family, they now have begin rebuild their entire home all that they have lost. Jeff, Hayes, the girls, and the pets are safe and out Redding with the bare essentials.
the coming months they will need relocate, rebuild, and reestablish the home that they had created for each other and their three girls, Lydia, Abigail and Clarity.
All those who know Jeff know that made his living from his home. esteemed music producer, engineer, and artist, and put his whole life into his creative spaces. While was able save his completed works, sadly most his equipment was lost the fire, (not included picture below) including his beloved late mothers piano.
The funding the campaign will helping with all the costs moving, getting clothing, and everyday living expenses, along with replacing house furniture and studio equipment. Starting with the basics, from the ground up.
Please share social network sites Facebook, Instagram, twitter etc... with whomever you can, and remember that any amount helps Jeff, Hayes, and the girls rebuild their home.
This truly has been the most devastating situation, and continues for many who have lost their homes the Carr fire. When everything has been lost, gather together push repair, and rebuild. Jeff, Hayes, and the girls are fortunate have much support and are determined push through this difficult time with optimism and love.
Our family thanks you for the continued support.

behalf Nadel Ciarlo, P.C. posted Residential Real Estate Tuesday, November 25, 2014.
Condominiums have become popular form residential real estate ownership for many New Yorkers. condominium, residents have separate ownership their individual units, and undivided interest, shared with other residents, the common areas such hallways, elevators and building lobbies.
Condominiums are governed board managers. The board members are typically residents the building and are often serving without pay. Ideally, they should try resolve any issues amicably and maintain good relations with residents. Unfortunately, with some boards this not always the case.
condo board legally obligated follow the internal rules and procedures found the condominium declaration, the by-laws and the house rules. Like the board directors corporation, they are obligated act with prudent judgment making business decisions. Boards are also bound comply with the New York Condominium Act.
you believe your board not acting compliance with internal rules the law, your first step should speak board member, explain the problem and request that remedied corrected. that does not resolve the issue, you should write letter the board and keep copy for your records. Your request will carry much more weight you have the support other residents. Condominium boards are elected the residents and will more responsive organized group.
Litigation should last resort dispute with condo board. Litigation can expensive and you should keep mind that you have live the same building with the people you are suing. Nonetheless, serious cases where the board not complying with the law the internal rules the building, may necessary court.

The second ailment commercial web today Sectionitis. From About Us, Photo Gallery, the Section has been the de-facto way organize site. While the Section itself has been solid solution for organizing content, the side-effect, Sectionitis, has led the dilution great ideas and thought process that demands you create content for the sections, instead ideas that makes sense for your goals.
Sectionitis says, have these sections, how does your idea fit into one these sections? your idea doesn't fit neatly into one these sections, you must either mold fit, create new section. your idea bigger than section, then the only answer make completely different site.
What you have four great ideas? Sectionitis would lead you creating four sections for your site, one for each idea. Organized, yes. But now you've split your budget four ways, and the result four ideas quarter-baked.
I'm going provide example for this one. Mountain Dew has "Green Label Art" initiative going where they have independent artists create bottle labels. The website has sections total, ranging from Artists Events Gear. And course landing page that binds them all.
One section the site Draw Your Own label. Very cool idea. Not ground-breaking, but perfect for what they are doing. allows visitor "paint" their own label design. However, the interface and functionality lacking. feels rushed, and after-thought. The signs Sectionitis.
Instead creating section-based site, why not make this idea the site. Spend your efforts creating environment that encourages the visitor express themselves while learning about the other artists participating. Allow the visitor the ability change the canvas other Artists renderings, while giving them the ability draw well? "Events" section that needed any part the budget beyond easy-to use and post RSS feed?
Sectionitis makes every section equally important, that section important.
you are the business conveying pure information, then neatly packed boxes possibly the best solution for you. But you're the business well, then might serve you better keep mind that more likely that structure for site will the by-product great idea, rather than the other way around.

The Maasai are ethnic group people that live Kenya and the north Tanzania. They are well known for the way that they dress and because they live near some national parks Africa that lot tourists to. They speak language called Maa.
The Maasai are old nomad African tribe the group Nilotes who have always lived Africa principally the north Tanzania and southern Kenya. They were discovered settlers around the 19th century.
They live near the african savannah animal reserves, for example the Masaai Mara reserve and the Serengeti.
The women build houses called Manyatta; the men build fence around the village protect them from lions.
The Masaai people live with animals because they are pastrolists. The more cattle Maasai man has, the richer is. They can't live without animals because their wealth comes from them The Masaai drink cow's blood because they believe they will stronger.
The Maasai speak Maa,very similar pronuncuation their Nilotic counterparts, the Luo and Kalenjin.
They are Christians, animists (their own religion) and some Maasai are muslim.
Physically, they are very tall and slim. Everybody wears bandannas and beaded jewellry, apply dye from dried cow blood.
They shave their women during marriages and their men pay lot dowry,in form cattle the bride's family.
During initiation,the boys their teen ages, jump over fire and kill lion order called men. There on,they can get married.
The Maa people have bad practice Female Genital Mutilation order make them 'women'. This bad practice this could make the young girls die from complications birth,over bleeding. But the government Kenya, Tanzania and NGO's have educated the locals over the dangers FGM.

popular one Aliexpress seems this "USB ISP" one, bought one. chose this one because has nice aluminium case, and pinout diagram imprinted the case, which handy. After having many one-off projects with bare PCBs collecting dust, now appreciate the importance having projects their own box case.
While has "USBasp" the item name, turns out that this was not USBasp device, and getting work like one takes some effort.
avrdude does not recognize the device, even after creating entry with the corresponding vendor/product ID. This particular device was designed work with their Windows-based called ProgISP and will not work with avrdude.
And apparently you can't just take the USBasp firmware and flash into this device, because the circuit somewhat different.
Disassembling the device simple. While grabbing the side the case, firmly push the USB connector inwards and the board should slide out the other end. You can then gently pull the board out the IDC connector.
The programmer seems based off the popular USBasp programmer, but modified somewhat (to what end I'm not sure). lacks some features offered other USBasp programmers, like the ability control the target's clock, use 3.3V for certain targets. But with nice aluminium case, what more can you ask for?
It's powered ATmega88 read that older versions were based ATmega8). The markings the board indicate that this MX-USBISP-V4.00. You can ignore tHe date because was never updated; the older V3.02 also has the same date. While the GreenPhotons blog was talking about V3.00, have verified that this version suffers from the same issue.
Note that there are provisions the PCB add voltage regulator, and the PCB link marked "C" can cut separate USB power from the rest the system. Link "D" can cut you wish disable target power. However, none these options were used.
This causes the USB line actively driven from PD3, thereby impeding communication to/from the USB host.
The rest this post will talk about (1) correcting this problem USBasp, and (2) uploading the firmware into your zhifengsoft programmer.
have been running software RAID array home for some time now. It's single network storage where consolidate all files. manage this array manually using the mdadm command. Some people choose buy NAS storage box which hides all the implementation details behind nice Web GUI, but it's essentially the same thing under the hood.
long the other drives the array doesn't develop bad sectors the same stripe, the data can still reconstructed from the remaining good blocks. This means that you can somewhat leave the drive for period without replacement, but course you are taking risk.
thought I'd share experiences with drive replacements thus far.
Most Linux distributions provide the raid-check script for periodic RAID scrubbing. This basically background cron job that tells the kernel start checking the RAID array. For RHEL/CentOS systems, this should occur every weekend.
During this scrubbing process, all drives within the array are read and their parity blocks are computed, ensure that everything tallies.

KARACHI: Driving Landhi you reach area the rather bumpy Mehran Highway where pick-ups and lorries number more than the other vehicles. Those coming are empty but the ones driving out are loaded with dozens silver milk canisters. Well, course, why wouldn't they be? After all, this Bhains Colony, the place the entire city gets its milk from.
You can pick some moos coming from various directions. But there are hardly any cattle roaming about freely here there. That's because they are all doing the work inside the dairy farms either side the road. The animals there eat and drink all day stay healthy and able produce lots and lots good quality milk.
"We milk our buffaloes twice hours 4am and 4pm," says Imtiaz Ahmed, one the staff supervisors Shaukat Mukhtar Farms. There are some 450 buffaloes the farm and almost all are lined enjoy their feed the men milk them manually hand.
"Eating keeps them busy. they munch their feed they stay one place for able milk them," says. ensure that the dairy workers don't get kicked the face the buffaloes, they also lightly tie their hind legs while milking them.
Asked why they don't have pumps for milking, explains that there are pumps too several dairy farms Bhains Colony, but their farms they prefer milk hand. "Usually, use pumps milk cows, not buffaloes. Cows give more milk, too. Pumps are good for milking quantity. But cows' milk thin while buffalo milk thicker and richer," explains.
The feed the animals eat has been specially designed and highly nutritious help them produce more milk. "It contains wheat, porridge, lentils, mustard, spinach greens, Malaysian palm oil, corn, ghee, etc," says.
The dairy workers with stainless steel buckets and metal canisters bring over the milk they collect pour into big metal tubs placed various points. "Fresh milk warm. Keeping big tubs helps cool slightly before sending off the market," shares Aslam Malik, another staff member the farm. And adds that the milk better reach the milk shops couple hours otherwise can bad.
"The shops have chillers and refrigerators store properly but here only concentrate the production part it," says, adding that the milk absolutely pure this point. "Forty litres milk carries about 4kg cream," says gesturing the layer cream floating top the milk the tubs. "But once this milk leaves this place, will tampered with and diluted according the conscience the people who are into this kind dishonesty," says.
The milk sold for Rs70 per litre the dairy farms and the shops sold for Rs80 litre.
"The price was only increased earlier this month from the previous rate Rs66 per litre," says.
The dairy farmers say that everyone should have fresh milk instead the packaged variety. "What you get the name milk box something devoid the healthy fats, cream and nutrition. processed milk where they have taken away the fat for making side products such cheese, butter, ghee, yogurt, lassi, etc," says Malik.
The dairy farmers buy new cattle every month replace the ones that have finished their milking cycle and are considered spent. These could then sent off the slaughterhouse farm where they can take short break before insemination and parturition before lactating again.
The best cattle, the farmers say, are from Badin Sindh and from the Ravi area Punjab. These may cost between Rs160,000 Rs200,000. They also have foreign cows and buffaloes such the black and white ones from Australia, which are known for producing more milk than the local varieties, but these are very expensive and delicate they require more care.

spite his 'odd' and 'quirky' appearance, the musician said was his differences that finally led his musical success.

said: hated primary school with passion. would cry every single day.

went primary school that was quite sporty and couldn't play sport and that was it. That was how you were cool.

Aside from the plot itself, the thing about Avengers that people have been talking about most what its actual title will be. Right now, all can place number next it, though all know that Marvel Studios give their films subtitles distinguish them. rest assured, there'll one place for the Infinity War sequel, too.
Originally, True Believers thought they'd with "Infinity Gauntlet," but it's probably for the best that that's been ruled out, we're being honest, it's bit too straightforward and mundane. Plus, Thanos has already acquired every Infinity Stone, you have admit that it'd seem redundant this point.
And though we're still waiting official announcement from Marvel, the folks over MCU Cosmic claim have very good authority that next May's blockbuster will called...wait for it...Avengers: Annihilation. you'll recall, the Russo brothers posted photo while back telling fans "look harder" and they might just discover the title. And turns out, was right there all along, you can see below.
Again, the studio hasn't confirmed this just yet, but would make sense given the devastating events Infinity War. Not mention that MCU Cosmic points out, continues the Avengers tradition "using titles big events the comics, although the comic Annihilation wasn't Avengers book rather huge (and awesome) cosmic Marvel event." Oh, and there's also the fact that it's long been rumored that the subtitle will one word only, and this certainly fits that description.
this stage, imagine Marvel will either come out and announce something officially pretty soon given that the title has leaked, switch and with another title (assuming Annihilation even what it's currently called). MCU Cosmic has pretty solid track record though we're inclined believe that now, least, this indeed what the studio's settled on.
Whether it'll remain Annihilation, can't say for sure, but with the first Avengers trailer expected drop November, should know soon enough.

From time time it's pertinent when discussing punting over the key factors which usually determine which horses will win and placed. Too many punters forget these 'basics' racehorse betting.
Summed up, can list them. as: Class, Consistency, Fitness. But added these are host other variables that you must also take into account along with class, consistency and fitness. These are the key factors.

Statistics show that few horses win which haven't started within the previous days. There are exceptions, course, because some horses race well fresh from spell, and some-like Kate's Myth-can come back and win after break months!
Always take into careful consideration whether horse has had recent racing. biased favour those which had their last start within the previous days.
Then there's the key factor distance suitability. horse may have class, consistency and fitness its side but placed over unsuitable distance unlikely win. Most horses are bred for certain distances and only few can mix sprints and staying races successfully. Check horse's past performances see suited the distance the race tackling.
Next factor weight-probably one the most important factors, because weight lynchpin handicap racing. The more weight horse has carry the harder its task; it's simple that. Weight can beat any horse, matter what its class ability.
horses win, perform well, they are given more weight. very wary any horse which asked carry 2.5kgs more the weight carried its last start--except when dropping substantially class.
Barrier draw also taken into consideration. most tracks, wide draw can severely affect any horse's winning chance. Take field 18, say, Randwick over the 1000m and you'll always see that those drawn outside tough. The same thinking applies other tracks and other distances.
Then there are key factors like jockeys, trainers, horse's price, and whether not likely suited the actual pace the race. It's good idea have key factor chart front you when you assess any race.
the horse racing form, closely approaching his best form?
the horse reasonably well weighted?
Can the horse handle the track?
Are the track conditions suitable?
the horse drawn well the barrier?
the horse the hands successful trainer?
the horse racing its correct Class?
well the key factors concerning the horses themselves, there the further issue choosing the right races which bet. Are you going bet them all, just couple, half dozen, maybe just three four? Some races are difficult analyse because poor quality, too much quality, simply lack form (early 2yo. races, for example).
Finally, there's the matter exactly how you about selecting horse win race. Quite often, it's most useful exercise find all the horses which cannot win! Get them out the way first and then concentrate the rest the field.
You this looking firstly all horses listed from 20/1 out 100/1, whatever the longest price quoted for the race. You can usually eliminate about one third the runners, which then n-takes your task far easier find the main chances.
field can usually cut down just runners and this where you can rigidly apply the key factors have already discussed this article. Look for horses running unsuitable distances, horses with big weight rises, horses drawn badly, horses which don't seem like the track, the going, horses ridden ordinary jockeys and those from stables which rarely win.
This second procedure should eliminate five the remaining runners, leaving you with the prime list winning candidates. this what God means spiritual blindness? first, you think you can manage okay other senses provide clues; but suddenly something shifts and you are thrown off balance. Second the warning: "But this not true the wicked. They are like worthless chaff, scattered the wind. They will condemned the time judgment." (1:4) Most would not quickly claim spiritual trees but none wants chaff either. So, what do? How can improve our spiritual eyesight?

During the segment, O'Neill took Fox's Peter Doocy his hometown Butte, Montana, and detailed the mental and physical preparation that went into the May 2011 mission. The first part the two-part interview with Fox News aired Tuesday evening.
The team was not explicitly told that they would raiding bin Laden's compound, and were only given vague details about what they would doing. O'Neill said that realized the mission's target when few the names that and his fellow SEALs were given "didn't make sense."
The more trained it, the more realized...this going one-way mission. We're going and we're not going come back. We're going die when the house blows up. We're going die when blows up. are going there too long and we'll get arrested the Pakistanis and we're going spend the rest our short lives Pakistan prison.
O'Neill said that had just returned from his 11th tour when was called back carry out the mission. was initially supposed lead smaller security team outside the compound, but managed convince leaders that would remain the team that SEALs called the "martyrs' brigade."
detailed his mental preparation leading the raid Doocy, saying that wrote letters his kids explaining that was going "the most important mission since Washington crossed the Delaware." Although O'Neill said that the mission was worthy him sacrificing his life, struggled with saying goodbye his family. "It was sad. There were few times when tears hit the pages."
The last person that O'Neill called was his father, thanking him for everything that had done. The segment included interview with O'Neill's father, who choked remembered what could have been his last conversation with his son.
Though the interview certainly powerful, U.S. special ops leaders have criticized O'Neill for breaking his silence about the mission. The Naval Special Warfare Command has issued statement asking SEALS remember their code, which dictates that they keep mum about missions.

uncover some the challenges and innovations occurring CNS, (BPR​) spoke with Raj Mehra (RM​), CEO Seelos Therapeutics, clinical-stage company focused therapeutics for such disorders.
RM:​ Some the biological approaches have been proven ineffective, not because efficacy but because the drug delivery systems are not able get enough the drugs across the blood-brain barrier.
approach that has been advanced the vector system, that's one way get delivery where you want [to the CNS]. However, has some side effects issues that need get flushed out the next few years.
The other approach that looks very promising, and few have explored until now, getting the drugs administered intranasally.
Whether the drug liquid formulation solid, you can practically put those [formulations] device that can [be administered an] intranasal spray.
What we've seen that one the quickest and fastest ways get the drug into the CNS bypassing the effect, and smaller dose can provide much better efficacy. Fast-acting drugs delivered intranasally are going big trend.
RM:​ People did not think about it, people thought that drug not orally available, "How about coming with subcutaneous delivery come out with patch?" The patches had mixed records, but subcutaneous delivery has decent record.
When people tried address the CNS space, they realized its limitations. you were antibody approach [to address the CNS then] you'd need large amount dosing assume that .1% will eventually cross the blood-brain barrier. So, that approach limitation what made people start thinking: "Can pursue other approaches the intranasal formulation?", and people saw remarkable efficacy.
One the approaches address aggregates the CNS disease antibody approach which are large biologicals.
The issue was when you have antibody approach, they have created antibodies that are very specific disease aggregates, only leaving the monomers alone which are needed for the body, and contains and reduces these aggregates (i.e. you can have potential for disease like Parkinson's).
That [disease modifying potential] have been waiting and hoping for forever. The issue that saw there that antibodies not cross the blood-brain barrier easily; you were come with 20g dose, you hope get 20mg into the CNS.
So, that's one approach and the other approach small molecule approach. are going pursue small molecule approach where you don't need the high dose, and the delivery mechanism could either intranasal could vector approach but these things need worked upon.
But that's what are looking forward because until now all the therapies available for Parkinson's were just treating the symptoms none them were stopping the disease halting the progression. Now understand but the challenge [remains] the delivery mechanism.
RM:​ It's becoming more like drug and device you don't just have pill that you deliver orally, you have drug that needs formulated for device.
the formulation side, always comes back the same old issue: your drug small molecule than it's relatively easy fit into the transporters because your drug either water-soluble, not soluble but you can come with nanoparticles​ and new technologies that can make palatable for transported the CNS.
However, the biologics will have some challenges. These transporter systems [needed for the CNS innovations], generally speaking, have limitation you can't have big biologic easily available transported.
So, the challenge will most the work that should done the CNS side for formulation coming with relatively small footprint the drug that can easily put into the vehicle.

Who what are NRTLs and why should care?
Well the Nationally Recognized Testing Laboratory (NRTL)1 Program managed the Occupational Safety Health Administration (OSHA)2. OSHA was created 1970 Congress with the mission overseeing working conditions and training the United States. The majority OSHA's regulations fall into the category general requirements, when came specific products, rather than regulate them directly, they decided create the NRTL Program.
Under this program, OSHA approves private sector organizations performs product certification. Underwriters Laboratories (UL) just one these OSHA approved NRTLs. There are many others, each with their own range (or scope) products that they have been authorized oversee. Depending the product category, there may multiple companies with overlapping scopes approval, meaning you may have more options than just UL.
Once product has been certified, the NRTL authorizes the manufacturer use their registered certification mark the product. While each NRTLs mark unique, they are all registered with OSHA. result their presence signifies compliance with appropriate safety standards.
why should you care...well, because means that there are usually more than one compliance company the game. Sure the biggest but there are plenty other, sometimes better, options out there worth exploring. Take look the list below see some the options available.

Will you demand that your team stays and completes all their tasks?

Will you offer shoulder cry counselor offer help?
Will you your best ensure that they're taken care of?
How you respond those questions will determine how your team sees you.

Let your team time grieve: Losses weigh heavy people. Their hearts are broken and spirits crushed. Don't rush them back work. you do, they'll probably use you anyways during their time grief.
Let your team know there are counselors available: Pain, death, and sickness drain people. They're tired and don't know where go. Sometimes they need guidance and encouraging friend move them towards therapy counseling. Find local, trustworthy organization that can help them get through the pain. And you can, offer cover the expense. Remember, healthy team member more productive than unhealthy team member.
Let your team know you're there for them: People spend most their day work. You and your team probably see more each other than they their family. willing let them know you're there for them doing the struggle. Have open door. willing listen. Allow them cry your shoulder. Offer your support.

Researchers have analyzed the outcomes millions games online poker they found something pretty remarkable: the poker players who won most often also lost the most money. How can that possible?
Online poker playing involves many many risky decisions some for small stakes and some for large stakes.
The simple explanation that those players tended win games involving small amounts money, but tended lose games involving large amounts money, the players who win most often are aggressive and stay many hands even they don't have the best cards.
Most the time this seems like good strategy because they discourage the other players from playing and they can collect pots with relatively small amounts money over and over. But, some the time the play aggressively when they have pretty good hand but someone else turns out have even better hand and then they lose very big pot money.
This raises important question: Why didn't these players will learn from those large losses? The answer follows naturally from reference dependence, dopamine neurons are very sensitive whether you win lose but are less sensitive how much you win lose.
So, when poker player wins $10 that coded good event the players brain effectively motivates them keep playing losing very large amount money say $1000 coded bad event but doesn't seem 100 times worse than the good its. Which means that winning small amounts and losing big amounts great way manipulate the activity dopamine neurons, but it's also quick path bankruptcy.
more positive note, very successful poker players lose most the time when but when they lose they lose small amounts money and when they win they win big. So, you can see why reference dependence one the big ideas and behavioral economics. This leads some the most striking, biases our choice behavior biases that can learn minimize order make better choices.
One the most famous such biases called the endowment effect. This bias has deep consequences for decision-making. Imagine taking apple and cutting off small slice off inside giving circle fruit about inch diameter. How valuable that little slice apple?
Perhaps very valuable you, but you aren't capuchin monkey. Capuchins are small and very cute New World monkeys that really really like apples. ingenious experiment Capuchin monkeys were trained exchange coin like tokens for treats like slices apple. Their exchanges were price-sensitive.
monkey might equally prefer apple slices and cereal cubes, both cost one token per piece, but the price serial cubes dropped say one token per two cubes the monkey would choose the cheaper serial cubes. The monkeys were first endowed with one type food like apple slices then given the chance trade for another equally valued food like cereal cubes.
The monkeys only traded about 10% the time, that they preferred whatever food they were randomly assigned. That's endowment effect. The monkeys overvalue what ever they have compared what they could get. course there might other explanations the monkeys might not like trade food all once they have.
So, the researchers ran the experiment again now allow the monkeys trade their apple slices for the best treats all: fruit roll ups filled with marshmallow fluff. Now the monkeys were willing trade about 90% the time. wasn't that the monkeys didn't want trade, they just show the endowment effect and overvalue what they already had.
It's tempting dismiss this example, after all humans are much smarter and much better decision-makers the pound Capuchin monkey. But, humans, the same thing even the endowment effect originally attracted the attention economists because helps explain some puzzling features market behavior, such the fact that people don't trade goods often predicted economic models.

little while ago Mini Reviewer, Izzie (5), won book token school and off went our local bookshop where she picked out new book all herself. was Isadora Moon Gets Trouble Harriet Muncaster. read together and that was it, she was hooked and wanted read the whole collection.
This book about Isadora Moon. She bit vampire and bit fairy. this story she gets into lot trouble with her cousin. They make themselves small with potion and her best friend, Pink Rabbit, made really small because jumped into the potion. They also make dragon and goes school with Isadora. She gets lots trouble school and sent home.
favourite part this book the dragon because breathes out stars and glitter, not fire!
now going read all the Isadora Moon books!
Isadora Moon goes school trip castle. The teacher didn't think was haunted but was. Isadora found ghost called Oscar and when she showed the ghost her friends they all screamed and ran away. Oscar was friendly ghost and was sad. Then they found suit shining armour and put Oscar it. Isadora took him back her friends and they all made friends with Oscar. Then Isadora Moon took Oscar's helmet off and they didn't scream. Oscar was happy.
like the front cover this book because it's all sparkly and like the pictures because they are all pink and black and white. favourite character this story Oscar because friendly.
think clear that Izzie Isadora fan, when discovered there was going Isadora Moon Halloween Party Waterstones with Harriet Muncaster, the creator the series, couldn't resist popping Manchester see what was all about.
are glad that did was fantastic afternoon activities and games. Our favourite part was meeting Harriet and learning draw some the Isadora Moon characters including bat, Pink Rabbit and even Isadora herself!
Izzie loved the opportunity meet Harriet and get all her books signed. She even won prize the fancy dress competition together with Poppy who was uncooperative, but very cute Pink Rabbit! Their prize was new Isadora book course and are now currently half way through Isadora Moon goes the Ballet and sure Izzie will ready share her thoughts very soon.
From parent's perspective, think the Isadora Moon series make perfect first chapter books for children around age and up. Izzie not quite reading independently, she reads bit (with help when needed) and then read the end the chapter. Reading together like this really building her confidence and she delighted reading "big" books! The stories themselves are fun and full magic but also with important underlying message, it's bit different, after all Isadora half fairy, half vampire, totally unique!
Harriet's eye-catching pink and black illustrations throughout the books make them very special indeed and think was the sparkly cover that initially made Izzie pick the first book the bookshop.
also want mention that Mini Reviewer, Theo, sometimes enjoys listening these stories too they aren't just books for girls! also enjoyed the Halloween event and was very taken with the miniature Isadora scene Harriet had created and could name all the characters, which thought was pretty good going considering still only three!
Thank you Harriet and also Waterstones for hosting such great Halloween party. Harriet appearing few more events November 2017, you can find out more about the events and all about Harriet her website take peek this fabulous interview with her over With Love For Books.
There are currently six books the Isadora Moon series and the seventh book Isadora Moon Goes the Fair will released March 2018. have little time read the others before then and I'm sure will!

you would like make this simpler form for younger kids, use wiggle eyes instead marker, simply draw the eyes for your children. You can also substitute felt construction paper for the wings, beak, and tufts hair. This would work for the feet well.
use hot glue gun for this project. You can use Beacon's 123 instant grab glue other fast dry glue you prefer. You can also use white craft glue but will need hold the wings, tufts hair and feet place the glue sets.
Cut cardboard tube desired size, mine are 1/4′′ high. Paint inside and outside cardboard tube with yellow paint and let dry.
Cut small pieces yellow chenille stems, about 3/4′′ long each. These are the tufts hair. Glue them together the ends that the ends meet and the tops fan out.
Cut pieces orange chenille stems, about 1/2′′ long. These are the feet.
Cut pieces yellow chenille stems, about 2′′ long each. Bend each one half, these are the wings.
Glue the tufts hair inside the top the cardboard tube. Glue the wings the side the tube and the feet stems inside the bottom. Bend them they stick out the front. See below.
Use orange paint add beak, and Sharpie marker add the eyes and eyebrows. Use old scruffy, small paintbrush dipped pink paint add the cheeks. Before adding the cheeks, dab off much excess paint you can that you are applying almost dry.

Don't forget about the video tutorial for cardboard tube lambs!

Social media isn't just about gathering many customers you can, like little prizes. It's about establishing and maintaining community. It's one thing for zillion people click "Like" your company's page, but they aren't buying your products, who cares?
Isn't better have smaller community who talks each other and with which you're able communicate? This can lead better environment that allows you bring repeat business. Anything else just numbers game.
One the best ways foster online community This includes getting your followers track you across several forms social media. Are they Twitter? Send them over your Facebook. Are they Facebook? Include link your website's blog and ask them comment.
What this does involve them several different ways. Not only they feel like they know everything about your company being top all the news and conversations, they also are constantly reminded your company every time they log their different accounts.
Another important thing remember vary your content. The more things that foster discussion the better. When people talk, they stick around. they stick around, they're more likely buy more your product!
Each day think the best content put out for your customers. Maybe few links here, some questions here, little humor there. The best content the stuff that gets people's brains going during routine workday.
Maybe even start your own online game puzzle? This really promotes interactivity not only with your company but between all your customers well. Consider shows like Lost each week episode would air and millions viewers would online and talk about what they learned what they thought. This would continue beyond the season and well through the summer.
All the while, that show was their minds. some new product came out, you better believe was snatched up! Consider what you can implant your company into customer's minds and fostering online community sure follow.
Who you think has the best online community right now?

Names can incredibly sensitive and important some people. Many people would take offense having their names mispronounced, even having their names spelled wrongly emails, text messages paper notes.
And no, I'm not referring the wildly inaccurate names that baristas from Starbucks write your cups.
The connection between Asian countries and western countries has brought about sharing cultural knowledge, which good thing. opens our eyes world beyond our own.
the same time, this brings about subtle changes regarding something that gives our sense identity: our names. Through western influences, many Asians have adopted the use English names instead those that are their native language. This done for practical reasons, such communicating for business deals and social media.
Asian, and immigrated Singapore from Hong Kong when was years old. Seeing that I've lived Singapore almost entire life, think have pretty good grasp the culture here.
Over here, mostly speak English because helps people all races and ethnicities communicate. course, all still manage speak our native languages, thanks the school curriculum.
the same time, however, Singaporeans have also developed special variation English called "Singlish." mixture dialects and English, and it's something that's unique Singapore.
Despite the deep Asian roots that every race here still lovingly keeps and remembers, many people are identifying themselves using their English names instead their Asian ones. Why this the case?
Well, for starters, English names are easier pronounce and remember. This helps both work and social life. People can remember you more easily, and this opens whole new world opportunities.
The psychology behind names very real. However, should made clear that having good name doesn't determine your success. When comes down it, your talents, personality, morals and values are the determinants your journey and successes life.
There kind distinctiveness the names all languages. These names remind our origins and motherland, and the rich diversity and culture that our ethnicities have. These names not set apart.
Instead, they serve open people's mind and hearts new things, such food, cultural practices, fashion and entertainment. They help want understand other ethnicities better. They help understand that shouldn't set people apart based ethnicities, and that are all from the same human race.
It's comforting know that some people here still maintain their Asian names, and introduce themselves such. For some, this because their English names are tricky pronounce. Introducing themselves with their Asian names easier because they don't have repeat their English names more than times order for people get them right. Others simply not have English names (and have desire have them, either).
someone who has succumbed the need for English name, always feel stifled when people call "Pamela." feel I'm not myself, and that another human being called "Pamela."
felt though lost identity and Asian pride that English name. Some people may deem this silly, but the fact is, parents only gave this English name because they believed would "help life."
Therefore, the government imprinted identity card when was years old. But since have used Asian name all the years leading the day got identity card, have already found both myself and voice Asian name.
Admittedly, Asian name uncommon even Asian terms and this has made more difficult remember and pronounce. Most the time, have repeat name spell out for people.
remember that when was younger, got teased lot for having Asian name because again, was uncommon. actually find myself having more appreciation for those who call Asian name because shows that the person was thoughtful enough remember uncommon and "weird" name.
understand that having English name would "easier." But the fact that it's easier should not discount the importance Asian names, especially for those who actually prefer addressed them.
After all, more common read and speak the language the country you're from. For example, people China speak mainly Mandarin, while people Japan speak mainly Japanese.
However, Singaporeans mainly speak English. So, that way, felt like part ethnicity was inevitably getting eroded and diffused. It's almost I'm not truly Chinese anymore.
I'm proud Asian name, and wish people would ask for preferred name instead assuming that I'd rather called English one. I'm proud Asian because Asians have such incredible culture and heritage.
And most all, I'm proud Asian because it's identity. This who am.

you are low groceries, not wait for your mom roommate buy them. Make the list and the stores yourself. you decline when your friends out dinner? you lock yourself the room when unexpected company arrives? so, stop doing immediately. You must never isolate yourself and hide away because you think people don't want you around, because you are nervous. Get out there and start living life.
Chatting with someone who shares similar interests one the best ways stop feeling miserable and lonely. your friends hate your favorite show, why not discuss with someone who likes it? Music can heal people from all types pain. you feel like people don't understand you, listen your favorite songs. They will help you realize that even celebs have coped with the same problems that plague your life. All are more similar than you'd think.
Texting your friends won't help you stop feeling lonely. Use your phone for its initial purpose. When you feel lonely, just call your family members friends. It's always better hear genuine laugh instead seeing plenty emoticons.
person tries begin conversation with you, not escape from them. your relative friend texts you, don't forget respond. However, make sure you not give one word answers. Think way keep conversation going. The longer you talk somebody, the less you will feel lonely.
Experience and money are the major reasons why get jobs. But there are also social benefits. When work with the same people every day, usually get know them well, and some coworkers become our good friends. Sure, you will not like all your coworkers, but you might find your best friend there.
Never allow your loneliness get all the best you. You only live once, make sure you enjoy each moment your life. What you usually when you feel lonely? Share your tips with us, please!

The Elder Scrolls Should Have Voiced Protagonist?
might not good idea continue with voiced protagonists.
One the big changes Bethesda made the Fallout series with Fallout and indeed, was change from routine for all Bethesda games was their decision with voiced protagonist. The voiced protagonist led to, Bethesda's words, dynamic dialog system, that relied centrally the (disappointing, even Bethesda's admission) dialog wheel.
But could have worked even without the dialog wheel- after all, games such The Witcher have had voiced protagonists for very long time, and they don't have dumbed dow dialog wheels. the question, suppose, is, voiced protagonist itself worth enough that the next Elder Scrolls games should look into bucking tradition and including one?
want honestly say no- the point Elder Scrolls (and Fallout) full escapist fantasy that the player can immerse themselves entirely into, projecting themselves into the role the character they are playing. Full dialog options, voiced only the player inside their heads, work far better for that- the character then sounds like whatever the player wants them sound like, instead sounding what they sound like, and the player having put with it.
There also the simple question the fact that the more lines are voiced, the more expensive the game becomes- meaning the more the temptation becomes have all least most dialog options lead the same similar resultant dialog. This was, fact, something saw Fallout too.
are hoping for The Elder Scrolls not dumbed down Fallout was, must also, corollary, hope that the new game doesn't have voiced protagonist- otherwise, we'll probably end with game unsatisfying Fallout ultimately was.

It's always bit downer coming back from holiday especially when you return from sunshine every day howling winds and torrential rain! However, did have something nice look forward our annual trip see Shakespeare the Castle. This year Stafford Castle they are doing Romeo and Juliet the open air. (The audience are under cover the actors aren't!) Janet and and two her friends from the Uni always and usually fun night.
happily got ourselves ice lollies and settled down our seats for another, what was sure be, great performance. people were being seated the cast was the stage playing music and dancing. was very jolly, summery atmosphere.
Then came tine for the play begin, the audience went silent and the young bloods from the Capulet and Montague families commenced insulting each other and fighting. Then started rain. got heavier and heavier until the stage was awash with water, not very safe for fighting and running about. After about ten minutes there came announcement that the play would have suspended temporarily. The actors left the stage and the audience sat and watched the rain.
Lady Capulet's brolly matched her dress!
After few minutes four stage hands came with brushes and tried brush the water off the stage. This provided some entertainment for while but the rain was getting even heavier and they were making very little difference. They eventually gave up, but had another announcement say that the performance would carry on. much applause and laughter Juliet, her nurse and her mother, Lady Capulet came out with umbrellas!
They gamely carried with their lines but you could hardly hear them for the drumming the very persistent rain. Everyone groaned and gasped when Juliet had sit down very wet bench, and Lady C's dress was starting cling her. Just the masked ball was about start, Romeo and friends arrived see-through waterproof capes (much everyone's amusement) had another announcement stopping the play. sat and chatted for around minutes, looking the sky for signs brightness but avail.
Then the stage manager came onto the stage with microphone and told that was going have pull the show conditions were just too bad.
were disappointed but could totally understand the decision. was way too wet and dangerous underfoot for them carry on. was the first time I'd seen Romeo and Juliet where the two never actually meet!
Just one put down experience! have been going for many years and this has never happened before. What summer!
Anyway the next day (Tuesday) was back work. had lovely morning and got few hugs too from customers that haven't seen for long time. was great see the Lace Guild again their final session until after the summer. They will back September ;-) was pleased that managed remember how get back into the new system! Had one two perplexed moments tried give borrower new card, and try and put message another one's record but Tracy and worked out the end! The morning just flew by!

There's common mantra the business world: "Hire slow; fire fast." The phrase catchy because it's basically common sense: take your time make the right decision, and you realize you're wrong, you should cut your losses quickly!
Well, agree with the latter part the mantra. you have someone who isn't the right fit and you haven't been able help them assimilate your organization's way doing business, you might need let them go. just hope that you humanely.
But want focus the hiring process. ideal world, you wouldn't need fire quickly because you would, paraphrase author and expert Jim Collins, only get the right people the bus. five years running Modify, I've only had let few people go.
It's easy copy and paste from old job description from another company's job description, but the reality that startup changes quickly, and that each and every business different from the company down the street. Examine each bullet your job description and make sure it's relevant the role you are trying fill.
For some roles, Craigslist right. For others, maybe AngelList LinkedIn. And for some, you'll need hire executive recruiter get the best results. Don't spend time weeding through bunch candidates who aren't fit. always, targeted search more efficient one. Pro tip: it's even better you can get referrals from your team and network. All these people want you succeed, and it's likely they'll only introduce you strong candidates whom they believe will fit your culture.
Make sure candidates know how your company operates. Help people say "no" this opportunity isn't right for them. They should know how many hours you work per week, what the office vibe like, and what type decisions you value. soon possible, you want people who don't fit your culture self-select out, and you want people who fit your culture really excited about your company.
You care deeply about your mission, and you only want hire folks who do, too. the cover letter isn't well-written and personal, then buyer beware. Look for someone who takes time understand your business and shares how they can help. Avoid the drive-by applicants.
recommend giving candidates project that fits with the role; resume can only tell you much. The "homework" can take day. Candidates who don't take this step, who haphazard way, probably aren't very passionate about the role.
the candidate's homework checks out, talk with them the phone. Try find out how they respond failure and how they've grown over the years. simple 30-minute call will help you determine candidate's decision-making process aligns with your company's values. Continue give candidates the option say "no" the job sharing your "warts." Candidates who are excited about the challenges you face are more likely work out than those who aren't.
This may sound controversial, but think you should check references before the in-person interview. this point, you aren't too invested the candidate, the checks don't well, you can halt the interview process (as you should). From the references, you can also identify what press during the interview.
Train your interviewers and make sure everyone well prepared. Each interview should have specific purpose. recommend one two interviews focused skill sets (can the candidate the job?) and one two focused culture (are they fit?). truly interview for culture, you should have set well-defined values.
you follow thorough hiring process, you will build strong team and likely won't have fire (neither slowly nor quickly). Getting the right people the bus will save you and the employee lot pain while giving your business the best chance for success.
Aaron Schwartz founder and CEO Modify Industries, Inc., which designs interchangeable custom watches known Modify Watches. loves working startup ideas and has spent innumerable (happy) hours advising friends and former students how grow their ideas.

Steve agreed convey his condominium Betty for $200,000 written contract signed both parties. During negotiations, Steve told Betty that, although there was deeded parking along with the unit, was allowed park his car adjacent lot for $50 month. Steve stated that had reason believe that Betty would not able continue that arrangement. Parking was important Betty because the condominium was located congested urban area.
June the conveyance took place: Betty paid Steve $200,000, Steve deeded the condominium Betty, and Betty moved. She immediately had the entire unit painted, replaced some windows, and added deck. The improvements cost $20,000 all. She also spent $2,000 remove the only bathtub the condominium and replace with shower, leaving the condominium with two showers and bathtub.
August Betty discovered that the owner the adjacent parking lot was about construct office building and was going discontinue renting parking spaces. She also learned that Steve had known about these plans before the sale. She quickly investigated other options and discovered that she could rent parking block away for $100 month. the same time, she also found that, immediately before Steve had bought the condominium, the previous owner had been murdered the premises. Steve had failed tell Betty about the incident.
Betty has tried sell the condominium but has been unable obtain offers more than $160,000, partly due the disclosure the murder and the lack parking space. Betty has sued Steve for fraud.
What the likely outcome Betty's lawsuit and what remedies can she reasonably seek? Discuss.

Rose Evelyn Griffin's journals contain the written record her life missionary work ever since she and her husband were converted 1964. She has served outside the United States twice.
She always learning new things and many activities keep her busy, including organizing and publishing the journals her many experiences, and amazingly from the nine missions she served for the Church including two stake missions with her husband before passed away, and seven full-time single-sister missionary.
"The Lord has directed serve every one missions," she said. "I've always thought that you feel like you should something, then it."
because that tenacity that Sister Griffin's life filled with marvelous experiences and just her life full, the bookcases and cupboards her home are also full with bulging scrapbooks and journals her memories.
Eight those journals have been typed, printed and bound for her posterity, which includes six children, grandchildren, and She said raising her children has been her most important mission and she hopes they will benefit her meticulously documenting her life.
While suffering many health problems throughout the years, including rheumatic fever child and open-heart surgery and cancer adult, Sister Griffin feels that each minute gift. "I've lived through things that you wouldn't ordinarily live through," she said. "The Lord has given more time."
Sister Griffin, and her husband, Russ, joined the Church 1964 Arkansas after coming contact with member California while there for specialized medical care for their children. She remembers the excitement her husband when found out about living prophet. "He knew the Bible from cover cover," she said. "He had always hoped find the true church."
From then the Griffins made missionary work big part their lives. They served two stake missions that were essentially full-time proselytizing missions while living Arkansas.
After her husband died, Sister Griffin took bus trip around the country collecting family history information and ended Mesa near some her family. One Sunday her bishop approached her before sacrament meeting and showed her list people the ward who were qualified mission; her name was the top. asked her think about it. Before sacrament meeting was over, she knew was what the Lord wanted her do. She served the Montana Billings Mission from 1977 1978.
